hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of World Day for Safety and Health at Work, we choose a film that celebrates unity and acceptance in the workplace, as the characters in Hairspray come together to integrate a local dance show.
hero.blurb: Hairspray is a fun, energetic film that combines music and dance to tell a story of overcoming adversity. With its upbeat soundtrack and impressive choreography, this movie is sure to leave you smiling.

released.title: The Hitcher
released.reason: Released today in 2007, this horror thriller still delivers plenty of suspense and scares, with a gripping storyline that keeps viewers on the edge of their seats.
released.blurb: The Hitcher is a tense, well-crafted film that explores the dangers of picking up strangers. With strong performances from its cast, this movie is a must-see for fans of the horror genre.

birthday.title: Spaceballs
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Jessica Rue, but more notably Kari Wuhrer and Ann Magnuson aren't our pick,  Happy birthday to Ann Magnuson (born April 28, 1956), as she stars in this hilarious science fiction parody that spoofs some of the most iconic films in the genre.
birthday.blurb: Spaceballs is a side-splitting comedy that lovingly sends up science fiction classics like Star Wars. With its witty dialogue and over-the-top characters, this movie is a must-see for fans of Mel Brooks.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: a famously bad movie with terrible reviews, I Know Who Killed Me is a convoluted thriller that fails to deliver on its intriguing premise.
awful.blurb: I Know Who Killed Me is a mess of a film, with a nonsensical plot and poor performances from its cast. Despite its best efforts, this movie is a chore to watch from start to finish.
